spec_match.rationale: Touches core terms from the chromestatus summary but skips CPU, real-world — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button); controls include "All cached (unimodal fast)"; "All cold (unimodal slow)"; "70% cached, 30% cold (bimodal)" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb hints at one angle but the concept page foregrounds different terminology — coverage is present but framing drifts.

edge_cases.rationale: One failure-mode signal is present but most edge cases (errors, unsupported environment, empty/zero inputs) are not demonstrated.

references.rationale: No chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link found — the self-heal cleanup script cannot recover this folder if the slug ever drifts.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on distribution-detector
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for Enabling Web Applications to understand bimodal performance timings introduces CPU, real-world but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://w3c.github.io/navigation-timing/#sec-performance-timing-confidence

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on distribution-detector
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link to the see-also section
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md rule #3: every page must include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link so the slug-recovery script can recover this folder if the name ever drifts.

openQuestions.title: Reconcile the feature-index blurb with distribution-detector's framing
openQuestions.detail: The blurb on the feature index sets up one angle but the concept page foregrounds different terminology. Either update the blurb to match what the page actually shows, or surface the blurb's framing in the page's H1/lede so a visitor lands on a coherent story.

summary: distribution-detector covers the happy path of Enabling Web Applications to understand bimodal performance timings and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions.
